enonce.texte: Match the job interview vocabulary with their correct definitions. This exercise will help you understand the key terms used during a job application process.
images: A simple illustration showing a person in a suit sitting opposite a panel of interviewers in a professional office setting

questions.texte: Match each term with its definition:

sousQuestions.texte: Interview
sousQuestions.reponse: c) A formal meeting where a candidate is asked questions to assess their suitability for a job

sousQuestions.texte: Position
sousQuestions.reponse: e) A job or role within a company

sousQuestions.texte: Qualifications
sousQuestions.reponse: a) The skills, education, and experience that make someone suitable for a job

sousQuestions.texte: Salary
sousQuestions.reponse: b) The fixed amount of money paid to an employee for their work

sousQuestions.texte: Applicant
sousQuestions.reponse: d) A person who applies for a job

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences using the present perfect tense of the verbs in brackets. The present perfect is used to talk about life experiences and past actions that have relevance to the present, which is very useful in job interviews.
images: A professional woman looking at a resume while smiling, suggesting confidence and experience

questions.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct present perfect form:
questions.reponse: 1. have applied, 2. have worked, 3. has interviewed, 4. have never had, 5. have completed

sousQuestions.texte: I _____ (apply) for several jobs this month.
sousQuestions.reponse: have applied

sousQuestions.texte: She _____ (work) in three different companies since she graduated.
sousQuestions.reponse: has worked

sousQuestions.texte: He _____ (interview) many candidates this week.
sousQuestions.reponse: has interviewed

sousQuestions.texte: We _____ (never / have) a problem with that software.
sousQuestions.reponse: have never had

sousQuestions.texte: They _____ (complete) their training program.
sousQuestions.reponse: have completed

enonce.texte:
Read the following job interview dialogue between Mrs. Kamga (interviewer) and Paul (applicant). Then answer the questions that follow.

Mrs. Kamga: Good morning, Paul. Please have a seat. So, tell me about yourself and why you applied for this position.

Paul: Good morning, Mrs. Kamga. Thank you for receiving me. I have been interested in marketing since I was in high school. I have just completed my Bachelor's degree in Marketing and I am looking to apply my skills in a dynamic company like yours.

Mrs. Kamga: That's interesting. What experience do you have in this field? Have you worked in marketing before?

Paul: Yes, I have worked as an intern at a communication agency for six months. I have organized several promotional events and I have also managed our social media accounts.

Mrs. Kamga: That sounds promising. Why should we hire you?

Paul: I am a hard-working and motivated person. I have learned a lot from my internship, and I am ready to take on new challenges. I believe I have the right skills for this job.

Mrs. Kamga: Thank you, Paul. We will contact you next week with our decision.

questions.texte: Based on the dialogue, identify Paul's qualifications and experience.
questions.reponse: Paul has a Bachelor's degree in Marketing and has completed a six-month internship at a communication agency.

sousQuestions.texte: What degree has Paul just completed?
sousQuestions.reponse: A Bachelor's degree in Marketing.

sousQuestions.texte: How long did Paul work as an intern?
sousQuestions.reponse: For six months.

sousQuestions.texte: What professional qualities does Paul mention about himself?
sousQuestions.reponse: He describes himself as hard-working and motivated.

questions.texte: Analyze the reasons why Mrs. Kamga might hire Paul based on his answers.
questions.reponse: Paul has relevant academic qualifications, practical experience through an internship, and shows motivation and readiness to learn. His answers are confident and show he has the skills for the job.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify two strengths in Paul's interview answers.
sousQuestions.reponse: 1. He provides specific examples of his experience. 2. He expresses enthusiasm and motivation.

sousQuestions.texte: What could Paul improve in his answers? Justify your response.
sousQuestions.reponse: He could be more specific about his achievements during his internship. For example, he could mention measurable results or specific projects he worked on.

enonce.texte: Listen to the audio recording of a job interview (the teacher will play it). The interview features a candidate named Marie and her interviewer, Mr. Njoya. After listening, answer the following comprehension questions.
images: A woman sitting at a desk, wearing headphones and looking at a computer screen, simulating a remote job interview

questions.texte: Recall and organize the key information from the interview.
questions.reponse: The candidate is Marie, and the interviewer is Mr. Njoya. They discuss Marie's qualifications, her professional experience, and her strengths for the position.

sousQuestions.texte: What is the name of the candidate?
sousQuestions.reponse: Marie

sousQuestions.texte: What was the main reason why Marie believes she is suitable for the job?
sousQuestions.reponse: She has relevant experience and excellent communication skills.

sousQuestions.texte: Summarize the advice Mr. Njoya gives Marie at the end of the interview.
sousQuestions.reponse: He advises her to be more confident in her abilities and to provide more specific examples during her answers.

questions.texte: Evaluate Marie's performance in the interview. Would you hire her? Explain why.
questions.reponse: Marie has good qualifications and experience, but she lacks confidence. While her skills are relevant, she needs to present herself more assertively. I would consider hiring her, but I might recommend some coaching to improve her presentation skills.

sousQuestions.texte: What are the main strengths and weaknesses in Marie's interview style?
sousQuestions.reponse: Strengths: She has relevant qualifications and experience. Weaknesses: She lacks confidence and is not always specific in her answers.

enonce.texte: Prepare for a mock job interview by writing answers to the following common interview questions. Be specific and use professional language. You are applying for the position of 'Junior IT Assistant' at a technology company called 'Tech Solutions' in Douala.

questions.texte: Write a response to the following interview questions:
questions.reponse: Answers will vary, but should demonstrate clear, professional communication and relevant skills.

sousQuestions.texte: Tell me about yourself and your professional background.
sousQuestions.reponse: My name is [Your Name]. I have a Baccalaureat in Sciences and I have completed a certificate in Computer Science. I have experience in programming and computer maintenance, and I am passionate about technology.

sousQuestions.texte: What do you know about Tech Solutions?
sousQuestions.reponse: Tech Solutions is a leading technology company in Douala that provides IT services and solutions to various businesses. I know they have a reputation for innovation and customer service.

sousQuestions.texte: What are your main strengths and what can you bring to this position?
sousQuestions.reponse: I am a hard-working and motivated individual. I have strong problem-solving skills and I work well in a team. I can bring my technical skills and my willingness to learn to this position.

sousQuestions.texte: Where do you see yourself in five years?
sousQuestions.reponse: In five years, I hope to have acquired more experience in IT and to be a senior technician at Tech Solutions, possibly leading a small team.

sousQuestions.texte: Do you have any questions for us?
sousQuestions.reponse: Yes, I would like to know more about the professional development opportunities at Tech Solutions.

enonce.texte: You have just had a job interview at Tech Solutions. Write a formal thank-you email to the interviewer, Mr. Mbappe, to show your appreciation and to reinforce your interest in the position. Be sure to mention specific points from the interview.
images: A person typing on a laptop, with a cup of coffee nearby, suggesting a professional writing activity

questions.texte: Write a professional thank-you email to Mr. Mbappe. Remember to include a subject line, a greeting, a body thanking the interviewer and reiterating your interest, and a polite closing.
questions.reponse:
Subject: Thank You - Interview for Junior IT Assistant Position

Dear Mr. Mbappe,

I hope this email finds you well. I would like to thank you for the opportunity to interview for the Junior IT Assistant position at Tech Solutions yesterday. I enjoyed our conversation and learning more about your company's projects.

I was particularly impressed by the initiative you mentioned about the new software development team. I am very excited about the possibility of contributing my skills in programming and problem-solving to this project. As I mentioned during the interview, I am very motivated to learn and grow with Tech Solutions.

Thank you for considering my application. I am very interested in this role and I am confident that I would be a valuable asset to your team. I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]
[Your Phone Number]

enonce.texte:
Read the following paragraph describing a job interview. Identify three mistakes the candidate made and explain how to correct them.

Jean went to his job interview without preparing. He arrived late, wearing casual clothes. When the interviewer asked him why he wanted the job, he said, 'I don't know, I just need money.' He didn't have a resume, and he couldn't remember the name of the company. He also avoided eye contact and gave very short answers.
images: A person looking stressed and unprepared in a job interview, wearing casual clothes and holding no documents

questions.texte: Identify the mistakes Jean made and propose solutions for each.
questions.reponse: 1. He arrived late. Solution: Always arrive 15 minutes early. 2. He was poorly dressed. Solution: Dress professionally (suit and tie for men, formal dress for women). 3. He gave an inappropriate response about his motivation. Solution: Prepare a good answer about why you want the job, linking it to your career goals.

sousQuestions.texte: What was Jean's first mistake?
sousQuestions.reponse: He arrived late.

sousQuestions.texte: Why is his clothing choice a problem?
sousQuestions.reponse: It is unprofessional and shows a lack of seriousness.

sousQuestions.texte: Analyze the consequences of Jean's lack of preparation.
sousQuestions.reponse: His lack of preparation shows that he is not serious about the job. He will likely be rejected because he made a bad impression on the interviewer.
